name: analogyMapping
invariants: output.ok === false || output.result.message !== undefined || typeof output.result.sourceDomain === 'string'
output.ok === false || output.result.message !== undefined || typeof output.result.targetDomain === 'string'
output.ok === false || output.result.message !== undefined || (Array.isArray(output.result.entityMapping) && output.result.entityMapping.every(function(m){ return Number.isFinite(m.similarity); }))
_note: LENS_ACTIONS handler (registerLensAction). Dispatch key 'commonsense.analogyMapping'. CommonsenseActionPanel.tsx now parses each domain field as JSON {domain,entities:[{name,type}],relations:[{type,from,to}]} (free-text degrades to one entity) — it was wrongly sending bare STRINGS → source.entities=[] → 'must have entities' message; FIXED. Returns {sourceDomain,targetDomain,entityMapping:[{source,target,similarity}],relationMappings,systematicityScore,systematicityLabel,candidateInferences:[{predictedRelation,from,to,confidence}],coverage} — the EXACT fields the card reads (was reading {source,target,mappings:[{sourceConcept,targetConcept}],coherence} which the handler never returns → dead card; FIXED). Structure-mapping theory: greedy 1:1 entity alignment by type similarity, systematicity = relCoverage*0.6 + chainDensity*0.4, candidate inference from unmapped source relations. All scores bounded/FINITE (string inputs, no numeric poison). Not assassin-driven (no derived contract for this LENS_ACTION); authored spec pinned by server/tests/commonsense-lens-macros.test.js.
